acquisition method: data-dependent acquisition using the full MS/DD-MS/MS setup.
alkylation reagent: alkylation using 200 mM iodoacetamide (Bio-Rad Laboratories, USA) in 50 mM ammonium bicarbonate for 45 min at room temperature.
alkylation concentration: alkylation using 200 mM iodoacetamide (Bio-Rad Laboratories, USA) in 50 mM ammonium bicarbonate for 45 min at room temperature.
cleavage agent: Protein samples were mixed with modified sequencing grade trypsin (Promega, USA) in a ratio of 1 µg trypsin to 50 µg protein.
collision energy: Normalised collision energy was set at 27%.
instrument: the Q Exactive Plus Quadrupole-Orbitrap mass spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific, USA) with a Nanospray Flex ion source was used for analysis.
ionization type: the Q Exactive Plus Quadrupole-Orbitrap mass spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific, USA) with a Nanospray Flex ion source was used for analysis.
reduction reagent: protein samples were reduced with 100 mM tris(2-carboxyethyl) phosphine (TCEP) (Sigma-Aldrich, USA).
reduction concentration: protein samples were reduced with 100 mM tris(2-carboxyethyl) phosphine (TCEP) (Sigma-Aldrich, USA).
